title: ipheion lorentzii
intro: Ipheion lorentzii, also known as the autumn-flowering star of the sea or star of the Iberian, is a perennial bulbous plant native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Ipheion lorentzii are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used internally to support overall health and well-being.
